text:
MRI BREAST WITH AND WITHOUT CONTRAST — RESTAGING, LEFT BREAST AND LEFT AXILLA

EXAMINATION: MRI of the bilateral breasts with and without gadolinium-based contrast enhancement, dedicated left breast and left axilla protocol.

INDICATION: 39-year-old premenopausal female with known left breast triple-negative invasive ductal carcinoma (ER-/PR-/HER2-, grade 3, Ki-67 85%), stage IIB at presentation (T2 N1 M0). Currently undergoing neoadjuvant chemotherapy per KEYNOTE-522 protocol: dose-dense doxorubicin/cyclophosphamide combined with pembrolizumab, 4 cycles now completed. Restaging MRI requested to assess treatment response prior to transition to weekly paclitaxel phase. Baseline MRI at time of diagnosis demonstrated a 3.4 cm irregular enhancing mass in the left upper outer quadrant with one morphologically abnormal left axillary lymph node.

TECHNIQUE: Multiplanar, multi-sequence MRI of the bilateral breasts was performed on a 3.0 Tesla scanner utilizing a dedicated bilateral breast surface coil. Sequences obtained include axial T1-weighted, axial T2-weighted fat-suppressed, diffusion-weighted imaging (DWI) with ADC mapping, and dynamic contrast-enhanced (DCE) series with pre- and multiple sequential post-contrast subtraction acquisitions following intravenous administration of gadobutrol (Gadavist) 0.1 mmol/kg. MIP reconstructions and subtraction images reviewed. Background parenchymal enhancement: moderate (heterogeneous). Fibroglandular tissue: heterogeneous fibroglandular tissue bilaterally. No contraindications to gadolinium administration noted. Comparison: Bilateral breast MRI performed at diagnosis.


FINDINGS:

LEFT BREAST:

Primary Lesion: There is a residual irregular mass in the left upper outer quadrant at approximately the 10 o'clock position, 4 cm from the nipple, corresponding to the known site of malignancy. The lesion currently measures approximately 2.1 x 1.8 x 1.7 cm in greatest axial, transverse, and craniocaudal dimensions, respectively. This represents appreciable interval reduction compared to the baseline MRI measurement of 3.4 x 3.0 x 2.8 cm. The lesion continues to demonstrate irregular, spiculated margins with a persistent rim-enhancing pattern and internal heterogeneity on DCE sequences. The kinetic enhancement profile demonstrates a mixed plateau/washout pattern within the residual enhancing component, which retains features of malignant kinetics, most conspicuous on the delayed-phase subtraction images. However, the area of internal non-enhancement has expanded relative to the baseline study, with a larger central region showing absent or minimal enhancement interspersed among the residual enhancing tumor nests. This pattern is consistent with treatment-related necrosis and fibrosis replacing portions of the tumor volume. On T2-weighted fat-suppressed sequences, there is a T2-hypointense core with peripheral T2 signal heterogeneity, suggesting a mixture of fibrotic treatment effect and viable residual tumor. DWI demonstrates persistently restricted diffusion within the enhancing components of the lesion (ADC approximately 0.82 x 10⁻³ mm²/s), while the centrally necrotic non-enhancing areas show facilitated diffusion consistent with liquefactive necrosis. No new satellite lesions are identified in the left breast. No additional foci of suspicious enhancement elsewhere in the left breast.

Nipple-Areolar Complex: The left nipple-areolar complex does not demonstrate abnormal enhancement. No retraction or skin thickening is identified.

Skin and Chest Wall: No suspicious skin thickening, skin enhancement, or skin involvement by the primary lesion. The pectoralis major and minor muscles demonstrate normal signal intensity without evidence of direct chest wall invasion.

Left Breast Lymphatics: No suspicious intramammary lymph nodes.

RIGHT BREAST:

The right breast demonstrates heterogeneous fibroglandular tissue with moderate background parenchymal enhancement, expected for a premenopausal woman. No mass, architectural distortion, or suspicious focus of enhancement is identified in the right breast. No suspicious axillary lymph nodes on the right.

LEFT AXILLA:

The morphologically abnormal left axillary lymph node identified on the baseline study is again visualized. The dominant node now measures approximately 1.3 cm in short-axis diameter (previously 2.2 cm). There has been appreciable interval reduction in the degree of cortical thickening; cortex now measures approximately 4 mm (previously >7 mm). The fatty hilum remains indistinct, though the degree of hilar effacement has decreased compared to baseline. The remaining nodal architecture appears less distorted. One additional small left axillary lymph node measuring 0.7 cm in short axis is identified; this node demonstrates preserved fatty hilum and thin cortex, appearing reactive rather than involved. No matted nodal conglomerates. No level II or level III axillary adenopathy. No infraclavicular or supraclavicular adenopathy identified on the imaged portions.

INTERNAL MAMMARY AND MEDIASTINUM: No internal mammary lymph nodes identified. The imaged mediastinal structures appear unremarkable.

OTHER FINDINGS: No bone marrow signal abnormalities are identified in the visualized sternum, clavicles, or ribs to suggest osseous involvement. No pleural effusion.


IMPRESSION:

1. LEFT BREAST PRIMARY LESION — PARTIAL RESPONSE: The known left upper outer quadrant triple-negative invasive ductal carcinoma demonstrates meaningful interval reduction in size from 3.4 cm at baseline to approximately 2.1 cm in greatest dimension following 4 cycles of dose-dense doxorubicin/cyclophosphamide and pembrolizumab. The mass retains viable-appearing enhancing components with malignant kinetic features, but a substantially larger proportion of the lesion is now occupied by centrally necrotic, non-enhancing treatment effect compared to baseline. Overall morphology and kinetics remain consistent with residual viable malignancy intermixed with treatment-induced necrosis and fibrosis. The lesion has not resolved and does not meet criteria for complete radiographic response.

2. LEFT AXILLARY LYMPH NODE — INTERVAL IMPROVEMENT: The previously enlarged morphologically abnormal left axillary lymph node shows interval reduction in size and decrease in cortical thickening, reflecting a treatment response at the nodal level. However, the node remains abnormal in morphology with persistent cortical thickening and loss of fatty hilum, and cannot be characterized as radiographically normal at this time. Continued monitoring warranted.

3. NO NEW DISEASE: No new suspicious enhancing foci identified in either breast. No new lymphadenopathy. No contralateral breast lesion.

4. RIGHT BREAST: Unremarkable. No suspicious findings.

Overall, the imaging picture is consistent with a meaningful but incomplete treatment response to the completed dose-dense AC and pembrolizumab phase of neoadjuvant therapy, with residual enhancing disease present in the left breast and left axilla.
